message:
core: bound AccessPredictor history, harden MVCC WAL rollback, support non-contiguous ext4 journal extents

ffs-fuse AccessPredictor (crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs):
- Add MAX_ACCESS_PREDICTOR_ENTRIES (4096) cap to prevent unbounded memory
  growth when thousands of distinct inodes are accessed over the lifetime
  of a mount. The predictor now tracks a monotonic `last_touch` timestamp
  per inode and evicts the least-recently-used entry when the cap is hit.
- Refactor internal state into AccessPredictorState struct behind a single
  Mutex, replacing the previous bare Mutex<BTreeMap> layout, so that the
  touch counter and history map are always updated atomically.
- Add two unit tests: `access_predictor_history_is_bounded` verifies the
  cap is enforced after inserting more inodes than the limit, and
  `access_predictor_evicts_least_recent_inode` proves LRU eviction keeps
  the most recently touched entries while discarding stale ones.

ffs-mvcc WAL durability (crates/ffs-mvcc/src/persist.rs):
- Fix a critical correctness bug where a failed WAL append or sync would
  leave the in-memory MVCC store in a committed state despite no durable
  record existing. Previously the code dropped the store lock before
  writing to WAL and silently ignored WAL errors, which could cause data
  loss on recovery. Now the store lock is held across the WAL write, and
  on failure, a new `rollback_in_memory_commit` function undoes the
  version entries, physical COW entries, SSI log entry, and commit
  sequence counter so the store returns to its pre-commit state.
- The rollback function carefully pops only the last entry from each
  version chain if it matches the failed transaction's commit_seq and
  txn_id, preventing accidental removal of unrelated concurrent commits.
- Add two tests: `wal_append_failure_rolls_back_commit_state` forces a
  WAL write failure via invalid seek offset and verifies the store is
  clean afterward; `wal_append_failure_rolls_back_ssi_log_and_commit_seq`
  does the same for the SSI commit path and asserts the ssi_log and
  next_commit counter are restored.

ffs-harness conformance (crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs):
- Add `ext4_reference_image_opens_with_journal_replay_segments` test that
  opens the golden ext4 8MB reference image with journal replay enabled
  and asserts that at least one journal block was scanned, validating the
  end-to-end journal replay path against a real-world fixture.

ffs-harness ext4 journal recovery (crates/ffs-harness/tests/ext4_journal_recovery.rs):
- Add NonContiguousCommitted journal scenario that exercises replay when
  the journal inode has two separate extents (logical [0..2) at physical
  block 20 and logical [2..3) at physical block 40). This validates that
  the journal replay engine correctly follows extent boundaries rather
  than assuming one contiguous physical run.
- Refactor build_ext4_image_with_journal into three focused helpers
  (write_journal_inode_extents, write_journal_payload_prefix,
  write_journal_terminal_block) to improve readability and make the
  non-contiguous extent wiring clearer.

ffs-repair pipeline (crates/ffs-repair/src/pipeline.rs):
- Fix hardcoded metadata group assumption: ScrubWithRecovery::new
  previously always treated group 0 as the metadata-critical group.
  Now it dynamically selects the lowest configured group ID from the
  provided GroupConfig list, which is correct for filesystems whose
  primary metadata group is not group 0. When no groups are configured,
  metadata_groups is empty (previously it would have incorrectly
  contained group 0).
- Add two tests: `default_metadata_group_uses_lowest_configured_group_id`
  verifies that providing groups 7 and 9 selects group 7 as metadata
  with Eager refresh policy; `default_metadata_group_is_empty_when_no_groups_configured`
  verifies the empty-groups edge case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
